access_script: Ontario first responders (police, fire, EMS, corrections, etc.) can call 1-833-677-2668 for anonymous peer support. This helpline is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Ontario farmers, farm families, and farm workers can call 1-866-267-6255 to access free counselling support at any time. Intake is available 24/7 and no fees or paperwork are required.
Francophone women facing violence can call or text 1-877-336-2433 for free, confidential support in French (24/7). If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Former residential school students and their families can call 1-866-925-4419 for emotional crisis support at any time (24/7). If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Youth (29 and under) can text 647-694-4275, email askus@youthline.ca, or live chat at youthline.ca for confidential peer support (Sun–Fri 4pm–9:30pm). If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Male survivors of sexual abuse in Ontario can call 1-866-887-0015 for free, multilingual crisis support and referrals. Help is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Métis Nation of Ontario citizens can call 1-877-767-7572 for mental health and addictions support. This crisis line is free and operates 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

Individuals impacted by missing and murdered Indigenous women, girls, or 2SLGBTQQIA+ people can call 1-844-413-6649 for support. This national crisis line is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Muslim youth and others can call or text 1-866-627-3342 at any time for free, anonymous mental health support. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

If you plan to use drugs alone, you can call 1-888-688-6677 so an operator stays on the line to monitor your safety and respond in case of overdose. This service is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Text the word "SUPPORT" to 258258 to connect with Ontario’s distress text line (available daily from 2:00 PM to 2:00 AM). If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

Call 1-855-628-7238 to reach a nurse who will help connect survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence to the nearest treatment centre. This navigation line is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Call 1-866-299-1011 for confidential support and referrals if you or a senior you know is experiencing abuse. The Seniors Safety Line is free and open 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

Veterans, Canadian Armed Forces members, RCMP members, and their families can call 1-800-268-7708 for free, confidential counseling. This assistance line is available 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.
Victims of crime in Ontario can call 1-888-579-2888 for information, referrals, and support. The Victim Support Line is free and operates 24/7. If you are in immediate danger, call 911.

Ontario residents (age 15+) with a valid OHIP card can join the free BounceBack program. Call 1-866-345-0224 or visit bouncebackontario.ca to learn how to get started (self-referral or provider referral accepted).

Call 1-888-939-3333 for information on cancer types, treatments, side effects, and local support services. This free helpline is open to patients, caregivers, and anyone with cancer-related questions.
Ontario caregivers can call 1-833-416-2273 at any time for information, assessment, and referrals to local support services. This helpline is free of charge.
Call 1-800-226-8464 for free information and support related to diabetes management and resources. This service is available to anyone affected by diabetes.

First Nations families can call 1-855-572-4453 (Jordan’s Principle Call Centre) for help accessing health, social, or education services for their children. The call centre is open 24/7 and there are no fees.

Ontario residents with asthma, COPD, or other lung health concerns can call 1-888-344-5864 to speak with a Certified Respiratory Educator. This free helpline offers guidance on managing lung conditions.
Call 1-800-668-2437 for anonymous and confidential information on sexual health topics (STIs, HIV/AIDS, birth control, pregnancy, etc.). This service is free for all Ontario residents.
Ontario seniors (age 60 and over) can call 1-855-598-2656 for free legal advice on elder law issues (e.g., long-term care, elder abuse, health care consent).

Call 1-800-531-5551 for information about Ontario’s Employment Standards Act (e.g., wages, hours of work, overtime, vacation rules). This free information line is available to both workers and employers.
Ontario residents living with HIV or AIDS can call 1-888-705-8889 for free legal advice on issues like privacy, human rights, immigration, and income support. No fees are charged for this service.

If you have experienced discrimination in Ontario, call 1-866-625-5179 for free legal advice and help filing a human rights application. The Human Rights Legal Support Centre’s services are provided at no cost.

Injured workers in Ontario can call 1-833-461-2411 for free legal help with workers’ compensation (WSIB) claims and appeals. The Injured Workers Community Legal Clinic provides assistance at no charge.

Young people in Ontario (under 18, or under 25 if homeless) can call 1-866-999-5329 for free legal advice. Justice for Children and Youth can help with issues in areas like education, family, youth criminal justice, and more.
Small-scale residential landlords in Ontario can call 1-800-730-3218 for free legal information and advice on tenancy issues. (This service is provided by a specialty legal clinic at no cost.)

Low-income Ontario residents can call 1-800-668-8258 to apply for Legal Aid or to get legal advice over the phone. Eligibility for full representation is based on income (financial means testing will apply).

Landlords and tenants in Ontario can call 1-888-332-3234 for information on landlord-tenant rules and Landlord and Tenant Board procedures. Staff can explain forms and processes but cannot provide legal advice.

Call 1-844-343-7462 to request a free 30-minute consultation with a lawyer through the Ontario Legal Information Centre. This service is available to any Ontario resident, regardless of income, and no documents are required to access it.

Non-unionized workers who have been injured on the job or faced reprisals can call 1-800-435-8980 for free legal advice and representation. The Office of the Worker Adviser helps with WSIB claims and occupational health and safety reprisal cases at no cost.

Visit stepstojustice.ca to access a step-by-step guide to common legal problems in Ontario. The website is free to use and includes a live chat service where you can ask a law librarian or staff for help with finding legal information.

Non-unionized workers in Ontario can call 1-877-832-6090 for free legal help if they have been punished or fired for raising health and safety concerns. The Workers Health & Safety Legal Clinic focuses on reprisals and unsafe work refusal cases.

Call 1-800-387-0750 to report a workplace injury or to get information about WSIB claims and benefits. This is the main toll-free number for Ontario’s Workplace Safety and Insurance Board, and there is no cost to call.
Call 1-800-321-1433 to speak with a trained volunteer about arthritis-related questions. This free support line is available to anyone living with arthritis or caring for someone who is.

Call 1-888-473-4636 for free, evidence-based information on heart disease and stroke. The Heart and Stroke Foundation’s help line can answer questions about prevention, risk factors, treatment, and recovery resources.
Call 1-833-621-0728 for information and navigation assistance with hospice and palliative care services in Ontario. (The HPCO information line is staffed Monday to Friday, 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM.)

Call 1-844-859-6789 (Monday–Friday, 8 AM to 8 PM Eastern) to connect with an MS Navigator for information and support related to multiple sclerosis. This service is free and available to anyone affected by MS.
Call 1-888-664-1974 (Monday–Friday, 9 AM to 4 PM Eastern) for information and support on Parkinson’s disease. You can also email support@parkinson.ca for assistance.

First Nations, Inuit, and Métis individuals looking to quit smoking can call 1-833-998-8255 for free, culturally tailored support. Live chat and additional resources are also available on the Talk Tobacco website (talktobacco.ca).
Indigenous people in Ontario can call 1-844-633-2886 to reach Aboriginal Legal Services for free legal advice and representation. No fees are charged for their assistance.
Ontario consumers can call 1-800-889-9768 for free information about consumer rights or to report unfair business practices. This is the provincial Consumer Protection Ontario helpline.
Call 1-855-947-5255 to request a referral to a lawyer or paralegal through the Law Society Referral Service. You will receive a free 30-minute consultation with a legal professional.

Indigenous residents of Nishnawbe Aski Nation territory can call 1-800-465-5581 for legal aid services. (Services are free of charge, but financial eligibility criteria may apply for ongoing assistance.)
